schema.title: operational command / procedure
schema.symptom: observable signs that justify running it
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}

tuples.title: Manually FLUSH MemTables to disk
tuples.symptom: MemTable size near limit; rising write latency; want to capture WAL truncation point before backup; want to test compaction pipeline
tuples.root_cause: MemTables accumulate until size or count thresholds trigger automatic flush; manual flush forces immediate persistence
fix: FLUSH [path1, path2, ...] [TRUE|FALSE] [ON LOCAL|CLUSTER]
TRUE = sequential only, FALSE = unsequential only, default = both
Example: FLUSH root.ln TRUE ON CLUSTER
After flush, expect file_count{name='seq'} to increase and MemTable count to drop

tuples.title: START / STOP REPAIR DATA — fix timestamp disorder in TsFiles
tuples.symptom: Out-of-order data warnings in logs; cross-space compaction failures; query results inconsistent across replicas
tuples.root_cause: Sequential and unsequential TsFiles diverge in time ranges; corrupted or partially flushed files; mismatched indexing
fix: START REPAIR DATA [ON LOCAL|CLUSTER] (default CLUSTER)
Pause with STOP REPAIR DATA; resume by re-issuing START
Monitor compaction dashboard: file_count of seq/unseq should converge

tuples.title: KILL QUERY — terminate runaway queries
tuples.symptom: SHOW QUERIES shows queries with very large elapsedtime; CPU pegged on Query-Worker threads; queries time out for other clients
tuples.root_cause: Unbounded scan, missing time filter, or aggregating across too many devices
fix: SHOW QUERIES WHERE elapsedtime > <seconds> — identify long-runners
KILL QUERY <queryId>  — terminate specific query
KILL ALL QUERIES — nuclear option, kills all in-flight queries
Then ask user to add time/slimit/align-by-device filters

tuples.title: SET SYSTEM TO READONLY — emergency write halt
tuples.symptom: Approaching disk full; WAL throttle critical; or planned maintenance window; cluster cannot accept writes safely
tuples.root_cause: Need to prevent further data ingestion while diagnosing or recovering
fix: SET SYSTEM TO READONLY [ON LOCAL|CLUSTER] (default CLUSTER)
Free disk / wait for compaction / repair
SET SYSTEM TO RUNNING [ON LOCAL|CLUSTER] to resume writes

tuples.title: SET CONFIGURATION — hot-update config without restart
tuples.symptom: Need to tune memory / threshold / threshold during incident; cannot afford rolling restart
tuples.root_cause: Some IoTDB configs are hot-reloadable (memory proportions, thresholds, intervals)
fix: SET CONFIGURATION 'key'='value' [, 'key2'='value2'] [ON <nodeId>]
Verify with SHOW VARIABLES (for cluster-scoped vars)
If hot-reload not supported for that key, the command is rejected — must edit file + restart

tuples.title: SHOW DISK_USAGE — locate space hog database/region
tuples.symptom: Disk filling up; unsure which database or region consumes most
tuples.root_cause: Skewed device distribution or insufficient TTL pruning on a database
fix: SHOW DISK_USAGE FROM root.**  — top-level scan
SHOW DISK_USAGE FROM root.<db>.**  — drill into specific db
Set TTL on hot databases; consider region migration off node

tuples.title: DEBUG SQL — capture file scan trace for slow query
tuples.symptom: Specific query slow; metrics show high cache-miss; cannot tell which TsFiles are scanned
tuples.root_cause: Bloom filter miss / metadata cache eviction / TsFile sealing pressure
fix: DEBUG SELECT ...  prefix the query with DEBUG
Inspect logs/log_datanode_query_debug.log for cache-miss and file path entries
Use the file list to verify whether unexpected files are scanned (compaction missed?)

tuples.title: SHOW REGIONS — diagnose region distribution and leadership
tuples.symptom: Skewed CPU / disk across DataNodes; suspected leader unbalance; planned region migration
tuples.root_cause: Imbalanced DataRegion placement; Leader pinned on a hot node; new node added but regions not rebalanced
fix: SHOW REGIONS — review Status, Role (Leader/Follower), TsFileSize per region
Identify hot regions and trigger MIGRATE REGION to move them
Verify status is Running, not Removing/Adding
